# Isabelle's COMP110 Data Analysis: Student Learning Insights

<!-- This is a comment. Below, you'll see code for inserting an image. To make this image appear, update <custom-path>. To add an image, save it inside the imgs folder of this repository. -->
<img src="{{ site.baseurl }}/static/imgs/logo.png" alt="COMP110 logo" width="500"/>

## Data Analysis Overview

This project analyzes COMP110 student survey data to understand patterns in learning, confidence, and engagement. The results are visualized using Python and Seaborn.

## Key Findings

- Students generally reported moderate to high understanding of course material.
- Confidence levels vary more widely than perceived understanding.
- Most students found assignments somewhat valuable for learning.

## Conclusion

The analysis suggests that students who rated quizzes as more helpful also tend to report higher levels of understanding and confidence in the course material. The scatterplot shows a positive relationship between quiz helpfulness and understanding, indicating that frequent low-stakes quizzes may reinforce learning.

The distribution of responses also shows that most students rated quizzes as moderately to highly helpful, which supports the idea that quizzes are a valuable learning tool.

Based on these findings, increasing the number of short, low-stakes quizzes would likely improve student learning outcomes. However, there may be trade-offs such as increased workload for students and additional grading or preparation time for instructors.
